name: Compressed Melon, Prosciutto Air, and Basil Pearls
description: A playful, modernist take on the classic prosciutto and melon pairing — featuring vacuum-compressed melon, a savory prosciutto-infused air, and delicate basil pearls for balance and freshness.
keywords: melon, prosciutto, basil, spherification, modernist, compressed fruit, molecular gastronomy

winePairings.name: Vinho Verde (Portugal)
winePairings.notes: Light spritz lifts melon sweetness and balances salinity.

winePairings.name: Louis Roederer Brut Champagne
winePairings.notes: High acidity and brioche notes elevate the dish.

steps.instruction: Arrange compressed melon cubes neatly on chilled plates.
Spoon basil pearls around or on top of melon.
Add a spoonful of prosciutto air over or beside the melon for an aromatic finish.
Garnish with micro basil and a light dusting of cracked pepper.
Place melon cubes in a vacuum bag with white balsamic vinegar and mint leaves.
Seal under full vacuum to compress fruit and intensify color and flavor.
Refrigerate for at least 30 minutes before serving.
Simmer prosciutto in water for 10–15 minutes to extract flavor, then strain and cool.
steps.note: the prosciutto can be used in another dish, like risotto or pasta sauce for added flavor

steps.instruction: Add soy lecithin to the liquid.
Use an immersion blender at the surface to create a stable foam (air).
Scoop the airy foam gently on top of plated melon before serving.
Blend basil leaves and water until smooth, then strain.
Mix strained basil liquid with sodium alginate; let rest 30 minutes to remove air bubbles.
Using a syringe or dropper, drip the basil mixture into the calcium bath to form small pearls.
Let sit for 1 minute, then remove and rinse in clean water.
Store pearls in a small amount of fresh water until plating.
